text.title: Schema'd Compaction Template
text.summary: The exact YAML schema the compaction prompt must produce — fixed keys, structured values, no free prose.

section.title: Required schema
section.p: The compacted state is a YAML document with a fixed set of top-level keys. The compaction prompt instructs the model to fill each key from the conversation; missing data becomes an empty list, never a paragraph.

rule.statement: The compacted state MUST be valid YAML with these top-level keys: goal, files_touched (list), decisions (list), errors_open (list), refs (list of {kind, id}), next_action (one sentence). NO free-text "summary" field.
rule.rationale: Fixed keys make the output machine-checkable; missing one key is a hard failure the orchestrator catches before the agent acts on bad state.

example.code: goal: refactor auth module to use refresh-token rotation files_touched: - src/auth/login.py:42 - src/auth/jwt.py:88-110 decisions: - chose RS256 over HS256 (ADR-007) - rotation interval = 15 min errors_open: - test_login_invalid_pw — AssertionError: expected 401 got 200 refs: - {kind: pr, id: "1842"} - {kind: log, id: ".agent/results/pytest_4882.log"} next_action: implement refresh-token rotation in jwt.py
example.note: Every key fillable from the conversation; resume by grep on goal + reading files_touched.

example.code: summary: | Worked on auth, made some progress, will continue next session. status: in-progress
example.note: No keys, no references, no actionable next_action. The next session restarts from zero.

antipattern.description: Letting the LLM add ad-hoc keys ("notes", "thoughts", "todo") that drift between calls.
antipattern.reason: Schema drift means orchestrators can't validate; downstream tooling that expects 'errors_open' breaks when the model writes 'open_errors' instead.

section.reference: Compaction prompt that produces this schema.
